lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tower guard
lines.text: We are the tower guards – our business is our own!

lines.speaker: Tower guard
lines.text: You can't go up there. That's private, that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Who are you? Are you one of the new guards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm an adventurer.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looking for adventures - what else?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh my, oh my! What does it all matter in the end, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear. Darn and drat!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: We try hard to keep this town protected.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: But how can we do that when the Watchtower isn't working?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean it isn't working?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: The Watchtower here works by the power of magic. An ancient spell designed to ward off ogres that has been in place here for many moons.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: The exact knowledge of the spell is lost to us now, but the essence of the spell has been infused into four powering crystals that keep the tower protected from the hordes in the Feldips.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how come the spell doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: The crystals! The crystals have been taken!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Taken?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Stolen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stolen?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Yes, yes! Do I have to repeat myself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I be of help?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Help? Oh wonderful, dear traveller!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Yes I could do with an extra pair of eyes here.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: There must be some evidence of what has happened here. Perhaps you could assist me in searching for clues?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would be happy to.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Try searching the surrounding area. If you find anything unusual, bring it here. Try the bushes - I've read enough adventure stories to know that clues get caught in bushes all the time.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: I will tell the guards to let you past - that way, you can just use the ladder to get in and out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: That's typical, nowadays. It's left to us wizards to do all the work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested in your rantings.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Hmph! Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wizards are always complaining.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Complaining? Complaining!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: What folks these days don't realise is that if it weren't for us wizards, this entire world would be overrun with every creature you could possibly imagine. And some you couldn't even conceive of!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some questions about the search for evidence.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Hello again. Did you find anything of interest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, nothing yet.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear, there must be something, somewhere...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this [robe/dagger/armour/eyepatch]...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Let me see...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: No, sorry, this is not evidence. You need to keep searching, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have a look at these...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Interesting, very interesting.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Long nails...grey in colour...well chewed...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Of course! They belong to a skavid!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A skavid?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: A servant race to the ogres: grey, depressed-looking creatures, always losing nails, teeth and hair!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: They inhabit the caves in the Feldip Hills.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: They normally keep to themselves, though. It's unusual for them to venture from their caves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you suggest I do?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: It's no good searching the caves. Well, not yet, anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: They are deep and complex. The only way you will navigate the caves is to have a map or something.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: It may be that the ogres have one...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do you know that?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well... I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You need to be fearless and gain entrance to Gu'Tanoth, the city of the ogres, then find out how to navigate the caves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds scary...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ogres are nasty creatures, yes. Only a strong warrior, and a clever one at that, can get the better of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do to get into the city?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You'll need some way to make the guards let you in.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You could start by checking out the ogre settlements around here. Tribal ogres often dislike their neighbours... In the meantime, I'll throw those fingernails out for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't bother, then.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Won't bother? Won't bother!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Perhaps this quest is too hard for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I search the caves?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: It's no good searching the caves. Well, not yet, anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: They are deep and complex. The only way you will navigate the caves is to have a map or something.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: It may be that the ogres have one...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do you know that?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well... I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha! A robe. This could be a clue...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's some armour; it could be evidence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha, a dagger! I wonder if this is evidence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found an eye patch; I had better show this to the Watchtower Wizard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this? Disgusting! Some fingernails. They may be a clue though... I'd better take them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have already searched this place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, nothing here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some questions regarding the tribal ogres.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am having difficulty with the tribes.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Talk to them face to face and don't show any fear. Make sure you are rested and well fed, and fight the good fight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have everything under control.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Good, good! I will expect the crystals back shortly then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost something the ogres gave to me.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh, deary me! Well, there's nothing I can do about it. You will have to go back to them, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Hahaha! Small t'ing returns. Did you bring the dragon bone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have what you want.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Then you shall get nothing from me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When I say I will get something, I get it!

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Hahaha! Small t'ing has done it. Toban is glad - take this...

sections.title: Speaking to Toban after giving him dragon bones

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: The small t'ing returns; what do you want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek another task.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Have you arrived for dinner? Hahaha! Be gone, small t'ing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't find the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Small t'ing, you lie to me! I always says that small t'ings are big trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need a key of some sort.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: What do you want, small t'ing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek entrance to the city of ogres.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Hahaha! You'll never get in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll find a way, trust me.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Bold words for a t'ing so small.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could do something for you...

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Hahaha! This creature t'inks it can help me! I would eat you now, but for your puny size.

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Prove to me your might: bring me the bones of an adult dragon to chew on, and I'll eat them, not you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Die, creature!

lines.speaker: Toban
lines.text: Hahaha! It t'inks it's a match for Toban, does it?

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Where my gold from dat dirty Toban?

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Well, well, the little t'ing has got it! Take dis to show little t'ing is a friend to da ogres. Hahahahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have your gold.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: No you don't! If you want to impress me, get da gold I asked for!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got it yet.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Don't come back until you have it, unless you wanna be on tonight's menu!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the key!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Stoopid! Take another and don't lose it!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Oh yeah? What's dat den?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, I've got it right here.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Stoopid!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the key!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Are you trying to trick me? I bet you got it stored somewhere. Humans always do that.

sections.title: Speaking to Og after giving him Toban's gold

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: It's the little t'ing again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other tasks for me?

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: No, I have no more tasks for you, now go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Are you blind? I can see you got it even from here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Grrr, why do I bother? It's a good job I got another part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Are you trying to trick me? I bet you got it stored somewhere. Humans always do that.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Why you here, little t'ing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek entrance to the city of ogres.

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: You got no business there!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Just a minute... Maybe if you did somet'ing for me, I might help you get in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help an ogre?

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: South-east of here der is more ogres - the name of the chieftain is Toban. He stole gold from me and I want it back!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Here is a key to the chest it's in. If you bring it here, I may reward you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to kill you!

lines.speaker: Og
lines.text: Kill me, eh? You shall be crushed. Guards!

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: The morsel is back. Does it have our tooth for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I don't have it.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: Morsel, you dare to return without the tooth! Either you are stoopid, or wants to be eaten!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: That'll annoy Gorad lots! Heheheheh! Here's a token of my gratitude: some old gem I stole from Gorad, and an old part of a statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to knock your teeth out!

lines.speaker: Gorad
lines.text: You shut your face! I smack you till you dead and sorry!

sections.title: Speaking to Grew after giving him Gorad's tooth

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: What are you doing here, morsel?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I do anything else for you?

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: I have nothing left for you but the cooking pot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: You lie to me, morsel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: Stupid morsel, I have another. Take it and go now before I lose my temper.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the relic part you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: You probably got it stored somewhere, morsel! Humans do that a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the crystal you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: How dare you lie to me morsel! I will grind you up for my bread!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the crystal you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: I suppose you want another? I suppose just this once I could give you my copy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the crystal you gave me.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: You probably got it stored somewhere, morsel! Humans do that a lot.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: What do you want, little morsel? You would look good on my plate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to enter the city of ogres.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: Hah! I should eat you instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't eat me; I can help you.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: What can a morsel like you do for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a mighty adventurer, slayer of monsters and user of magic powers.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: Well, well, perhaps the morsel can help after all...

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: If you t'ink you're tough, find Gorad, my enemy to the south-east, and knock one of his teeth out! Heheheheh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will have to kill me first.

lines.speaker: Grew
lines.text: That's not tricky - guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An ogre called Og gave me this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An ogre called Grew gave me this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An ogre called Toban gave me this...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ah, it's part of an old ogre statue. I'll take that off you - it might come in handy if I can fix it up.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ah, it's more of that old ogre statue. I'll take this bit too.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: It looks like you've brought me all the pieces of the relic. It is as I thought - a statue symbolising an ogre warrior of old.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well, if you ever want to make friends with an ogre, this is the item to have! Now I'll try to assemble it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some questions about the ogre city.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a feeling you might say that.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard again

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ah, the warrior returns. Have you found a way into Gu'Tanoth yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't get past the guards.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well, ogres dislike others apart from their own kind. What you need is some form of proof of friendship, something to trick them into believing you're their friend.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Which shouldn't be too hard, considering their intelligence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic you gave me.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Have you gone mad? I can see you have it with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic you gave me.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: What? Lost the relic? How careless! It's a good job I copied its design, then! You can take this copy instead - it's just as good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the relic you gave me.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: I imagine you've stored it somewhere. Go and see where you might have put it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will find my way in, no problem.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure you will... Good luck!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Stop, creature! Only ogres and their friends allowed in this city. Show me a sign of companionship, like a lost relic or somefing, and you may pass.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Until then, back to whence you came!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Well, what proof of friendship did you bring?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a relic!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: It's got the statue of Dalgroth. Welcome to Gu'Tanoth, friend of the ogres.

sections.title: Talking to the wizards after gaining access to the city

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made it into the ogre city!

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's like talking to a broken record.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard after gaining access to the city

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: How are you doing with the ogres?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have gained entry to the city!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Already? Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still can't navigate the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You need a map of some kind... I bet one of the ogres has one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay thanks, I'll go and find out.

sections.title: Talking to the ogre guard again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... is there anything in my backpack that you'd want?

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: You didn't bring anything! Shove off!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Stop, creature! Oh it's you. Well, what have you got for us, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about this?

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Well, well, look at this. My favourite: rock cake! Okay, we will let it through.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Oi! Where do you think you are going? You are for the cooking pot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I am a friend to ogres...

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Prove it to us with a gift. Get us something from the market.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Surprise us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not if I can help it.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: You can help by being tonight's dinner, or you can go away. Now, which shall it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'm going.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Back to whence you came!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Grrrrr!

sections.title: Talking to the southern ogre guard

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: What do you want, small thing? Leave us alone!

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Oi! Little thing. If you want to cross here, you must pay me 20 gold pieces first!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to give you 20 gold pieces to let me jump off a bridge?

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: That's what I said, like it or lump it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll pay it.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: A wise choice, little thing.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: And where is your money? Grrrr! Do you want to get hurt or something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll pay it.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: A wise choice, little thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew! I just made it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it, I'm not paying.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: In that case you're not crossing.

sections.title: Talking to the city guard again

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Hur, hur, hur... I see you haven't worked it out yet. Keep trying, imp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I hear the riddle again?

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: I want you to bring me an item: I will give you all the letters of this item, you work out what it is...

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My first is in days, but not in years, My second is in evil, and also in tears, My third is in all, but not in none, My fourth is in hot, but not in sun.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My fifth is in heaven, and also in hate, My sixth is in fearing, but not in fate, My seventh is in plush, but not in place.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My eighth is in nine, but not in eight, My last is in earth, and also in great.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My whole is an object, that magic will make. It brings wrack and ruin to all in its wake... Now how long, I wonder, will this riddle take?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back!

sections.title: Using the wrong item on the guard

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: That is not what I want, imp!

sections.title: Using the death rune on the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I worked it out!

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Hur, hur. The imp has done it! This is what you be needing...

sections.title: Talking to him after having given him the rune

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other riddles for me?

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Yes, what looks good on a plate with salad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: You! Now go, and bother me no more.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A nice pizza?

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Grr! Think you are a comedian, eh? Get lost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the map you gave me.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: What's the point? Take this one and go away!

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: You imps always store things in places and forget. Go look for it.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Are you blind? What is that you are carrying?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that map...

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Grrrr, what business you got here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on an errand.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: So what you want with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am an ogre killer come to destroy you!

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: I would like to see you try!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek passage into the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: Is that so... You humour me small t'ing. Answer this riddle and I will help you...

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: I want you to bring me an item: I will give you all the letters of this item, you work out what it is...

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My first is in days, but not in years, My second is in evil, and also in tears, My third is in all, but not in none, My fourth is in hot, but not in sun.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My fifth is in heaven, and also in hate, My sixth is in fearing, but not in fate, My seventh is in plush, but not in place.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My eighth is in nine, but not in eight, My last is in earth, and also in great.

lines.speaker: City guard
lines.text: My whole is an object, that magic will make. It brings wrack and ruin to all in its wake... Now how long, I wonder, will this riddle take?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad that was easier on the way back!

sections.title: Talking to the wizards after getting the skavid map

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some questions about the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Oh, really? I'm quite an expert on skavids.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Only joking!

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard after getting the skavid map

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: How is the quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have worked out the guard's puzzle.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: My, my. A wordsmith as well as a hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am still trying to navigate the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Take some illumination with you or else it will be dark.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to get into the shamans' cave.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Yes, it will be well guarded. Hmmm, let me see...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ah yes, I gather some ogres are allergic to certain herbs. Now what was it? It had white berries and blue leaves....

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Cave nightshade, that's it!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You should try looking through some of the caves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: And don't forget to look out for the dragons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The WHAT?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Dragons.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Didn't you know that the ogres keep blue dragons as pets and for magical ingredients?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well, regardless, I'm sure a bold, brave adventurer such as yourself won't have any problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is going well.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: That's good to hear. We are much closer to fixing the tower now.

sections.title: Talking to the scared skavid again

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Master, how are you doing learning our language?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am studying the speech of your kind...

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Tanath cur, tanath cur!

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Don't hurt me, don't hurt me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop moaning, creature. I know about you skavids - you serve those monsters, the ogres.

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Please don't touch me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have something that belongs to me...

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: I don't have anything; please believe me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somehow, I find your words hard to believe.

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: I'm begging your kindness; I don't have it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't believe you, hand it over!

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Ahhhhh, help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh great, I'm not getting anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'm not going to hurt you.

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Thank you, kind [sir/madam].

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: I'll tells you where that things you wants is. The mad skavids have it in their cave in the city.

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: You will have to learn skavid, otherwise they will not talks to you. Here, I'll tell you what you need to know...

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: You shut your mouth! Don't tell them anything!

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Tanath cur, tanath cur!

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: I cannot help you, but the others can. Let me tells you the most common skavid words: Ar, nod, gor, ig and cur. Those will gets you started.

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Bidith tanath...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cur.

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Cur, cur tanath!

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Cur tanath!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ar.

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Bidith tanath...

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Cur bidith...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ig.

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Ig! Bidith ig!

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Tanath gor...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nod.

lines.speaker: Skavid
lines.text: Nod, gor nod!

sections.title: Talking to the scared skavid after learning skavid

lines.speaker: Scared skavid
lines.text: Master, my kinsmen tell me you have learned skavid. You should speak to the mad ones in their cave.

sections.title: Talking to the mad skavid again

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: What, you gots the crystal...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've lost it!

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: Alright, take this one then...

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: You stored it somewhere maybe. Go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay then.

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: You be on your way, then.

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: Gor nod...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tanath.

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: Hehe! So you speak a little skavid, eh? I'll give you a prize for that!

lines.speaker: Mad skavid
lines.text: The others are with the shamans in the enclave - you'll never get them back. They hid one in the sacred rock! Nothing can touch it now.

sections.title: Talking to the wizards after learning to navigate the skavid caves

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have gained access to the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd get more help out of a-

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard after learning to navigate the skavid caves

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Hello again. How do you fare?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It goes well. I can now navigate the skavid caves.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: That is good news. Let me know if you find anything of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a crystal, but I lost it!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh no. Well, you had better go back there, then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am now ready for the shamans.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Remember all I told you. You must distract the guard somehow. Cave nightshade is what you are looking for - try searching the skavid caves for it.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: This herb is very poisonous, however, so handle it with care. Also, be on your guard in that cave. Who knows what monsters may be in that awful place...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: And don't forget to look out for the dragons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The WHAT?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Dragons.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Didn't you know that the ogres keep blue dragons as pets and for magical ingredients

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well, regardless, I'm sure a bold, brave adventurer such as yourself won't have any problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great...

sections.title: Attempting to enter the Ogre Enclave before distracting the guards

lines.speaker: Enclave guard
lines.text: No you don't!

lines.speaker: Enclave guard
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to go in there.

lines.speaker: Enclave guard
lines.text: Oh you do, do you? How about 'no'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to rid the world of ogres.

lines.speaker: Enclave guard
lines.text: You dare mock me, creature!

sections.title: Using cave nightshade on an enclave guard

lines.speaker: Enclave guard
lines.text: What is this? Arrrrgh! I cannot stand this plant! Argh, it burns! It burns!

lines.speaker: Ogre chieftain
lines.text: Grrr! Go away from me, vermin!

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: Grr! How dare you talk to us. We will destroy you!

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: Hur, hut, hur. Stoopid dragon!

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: Take dis!

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: Hur, hur, hur, hur!

sections.title: Talking to the wizards after discovering the shamans are invincible

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The shamans are too powerful for me to attack!

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You'll have to speak with the Watchtower Wizard about that.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard after discovering the shamans are invincible
Talking to the Watchtower Wizard again

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Any more news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me again what I need for the potion?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Yes indeed, let me see, what was it again...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You will need a guam leaf. That goes into the mix first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the next ingredient?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You need some jangerberries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some of those.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: You will need to add those after the guam leaf has gone in, but before the final ingredient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the final ingredient?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: The final ingredient is ground bat bones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some bat bones; how can I grind them?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: A pestle and mortar will more than do the trick.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: When you have mixed the potion correctly, then I can empower the potion with magic and the ogre shamans can be destroyed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still working on it.

sections.title: Talking to the wizards after learning the potion recipe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any chance of advice about this potion?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Nope. Try the Watchtower Wizard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found the cave of ogre shaman, but I cannot touch them!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: That is because of their magical powers. We must fight them with their own methods; do not speak to them! I suggest a potion...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Collect a guam leaf, add jangerberries, then mix in some ground bat bones. You must return it to me before you use it, so that I can empower it with my magic.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Be very careful how you mix it as it is extremely volatile. Mixing ingredients of this type in the wrong order can cause explosions!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: I hope you've been brushing up Herblore and Magic? I must warn you, only experienced magicians can use the potion. It is too dangerous in the hands of the unskilled.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard after making the potion

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Any more news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have made the potion.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: That's great news; let me infuse it with magic...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Here it is - a dangerous substance. I must remind you that this potion can only be used if your Magic level is high enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How high is high enough?

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Well, if you can cast Bones to Bananas, or any spells beyond that, you should be alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's one destroyed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the second one gone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There goes the fourth...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's five! Only one more left now...

sections.title: Prospecting the Rock of Dalgroth after mining it
Attempting to mine the Rock of Dalgroth again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have this crystal. There is no benefit to getting another.

sections.title: Returning to the Watchtower Wizard after killing the shamans without mining the rock

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Hello again. Did the potion work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed it did! I wiped out those ogre shamans!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had another crystal but I lost it.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Oh, really? If you got it from someone, maybe they'll have found it again. Otherwise, go and search the place where you got it in the first place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for another crystal.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: I am sure the cave holds the final crystal. Look for the source of the shamans' power.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I will go and have a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found another crystal!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Good, hand it over to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any chance of a hand?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Well, you could ask the Watchtower Wizard for one.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard with all the crystals

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Hello again. Did the potion work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed it did! I wiped out those ogre shamans!

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Magnificent! At last you've brought all the crystals.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Now the shield generator can be activated and, once again, Yanille will be safe from the threat of the ogres.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Put the crystals on the pillars there and throw the lever to activate the system.

sections.title: Talking to the Watchtower Wizard again

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: The system is not activated yet. Throw the switch to start it.

sections.title: Talking to a wizard before activating the generator

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: You will need to speak to the Watchtower Wizard.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Seriously, he's the one in charge here.

sections.title: Pulling the lever before placing the crystals
Placing a crystal on the wrong pillar

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Marvellous! It works! The town will now be safe. Take these coins as a token of my gratitude. Also, let me improve your Magic level.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: I've given you a special scroll too - it's a new spell. Read the scroll and you will be able to teleport yourself here.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Greetings, friend. I trust all is well with you? Yanille is safe at last!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any more quests for me?

lines.speaker: Watchtower wizard
lines.text: More quests? No, indeed, adventurer. You have done us a great service, already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the scroll you gave me.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Never mind, have another...

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: Ho, ho, ho! A comedian to the finish. There it is, in your backpack!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay.

lines.speaker: Watchtower Wizard
lines.text: We are always in your debt; do come and visit us again.

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: All's well that ends well.
